cataloging_subject.label: The English Register of Oseney Abbey
cataloging_subject.scope_note: The cataloged unit is the edition-level composite represented by U-M item AHA2740 and the repository XML. Its historical text comprises two distinct components in The National Archives E 164/26: an unfoliated title and partial chapter-table fragment of Nicholas Love's Middle English Mirror of the Blessed Life of Jesus Christ, and an incomplete Middle English translation of the Oseney Abbey Latin cartulary now Christ Church D&C vi.a.1 (= MS 343), represented by 279 editorial/documentary entries in 33 title groups. Andrew Clark's 1907 text, his 1913 forewords and indexes, the physical composite host, digital page images, U-M encoding, and repository XML are separate transmission layers described in the linked lineage manifest. The host's later Latin papal-confirmation section at ff. 66r-112v lies outside both Clark's edited target and this metadata subject.

catalog_summary.title: The English Register of Oseney Abbey
catalog_summary.author: Anonymous Oseney Abbey translator or translators and documentary creators; Nicholas Love for the distinct prefixed Mirror fragment
catalog_summary.editor: Andrew Clark, with Latin-register collation supplied by H. E. Salter
catalog_summary.date: Mirror translation/adaptation by about 1410, surviving fragment copied about 1450; Oseney register translated about 1460
catalog_summary.region: The cartulary is associated with Oseney Abbey and Oxfordshire; the independent Mirror fragment is English, but its exact copy origin is unresolved

titles.value: The English Register of Oseney Abbey
notes: This established edition title names the encoded composite; it does not collapse the prefixed Mirror fragment and the translated register into one medieval authored work.

titles.value: The Mirrour of the Lyfe of Cryste
notes: The spelling follows Clark's heading. The encoded portion contains only a title and incomplete chapter table, not the Mirror's continuous text.

agents.name: Nicholas Love
notes: Translator and adapter of the Middle English Mirror represented only by the prefixed title and partial chapter-table fragment.

agents.name: Andrew Clark
notes: Editor of the two-part EETS edition published in 1907 and 1913.

agents.name: Herbert Edward Salter
notes: Provided Clark with a working Latin-register transcript and selected readings, including readings from Cotton MS Vitellius E XV.

agents.name: Documentary creators represented in the Oseney register
notes: A heterogeneous collective of issuers and other responsible parties represented by charters, confirmations, grants, agreements, and related records; this grouping does not erase item-level identities.

scope.description: The Middle English Mirror translation and adaptation represented only by the title and partial chapter table

notes: Modern scholarship identifies Love's work as a translation and adaptation of the pseudo-Bonaventuran Meditationes vitae Christi. Clark's Bonaventure shorthand and his John Morton attribution are retained only as historical edition-layer claims in the lineage record.

responsibilities.display_name: Anonymous Oseney Abbey translator or translators

scope.description: The incomplete Middle English translation of the Latin Oseney cartulary

notes: No named translator is established. Clark argues that the English register follows the order of Christ Church D&C vi.a.1 (= MS 343), with a small number of English deeds not found there.

scope.description: Item-level responsibility for the heterogeneous documentary acts represented in the translated register

assertion.notes: The collective role is a cataloging convenience; names and functions differ from entry to entry.

Latin-register comparison and selected readings used by Clark

notes: The evidence supports a material intermediary: Clark worked from Salter's transcript and communications, not a directly demonstrated autopsy of every Latin witness.

scope.description: Nicholas Love's Middle English translation and adaptation, not the date of this surviving copy

assertion.notes: Clark's approximate dating remains qualified; the current TNA catalog dates the whole composite broadly to the fifteenth-sixteenth centuries.

scope.description: Publication of the target text in EETS Original Series 133

places.label: Oseney Abbey
notes: The Augustinian abbey outside medieval Oxford is the institutional center of the register and its documentary archive.

Primary regional setting of the properties, rights, and transactions represented

scope.description: Historical title/chapter-table fragment and translated register

language_statements.label: English

scope.description: Clark's modern editorial framing, notes, grammar, and indexes represented selectively in the encoding

notes: Latin comparison readings and quoted formulas do not make Latin a whole-work language for this cataloging subject.

form_statements.description: The two counted intellectual components—the partial Mirror chapter-table fragment and the English register compilation—are prose; the 279 DIV3 entry units remain structural/editorial metrics rather than separately counted items. The XML contains no verse-line or verse-group elements.

scope.description: The incomplete Middle English register translation and its 279 documentary/editorial entries
Title and partial chapter-table fragment of Nicholas Love's translation/adaptation

summaries.value: An edition-level composite preserving two distinct Middle English prose components from TNA E 164/26: a title and incomplete chapter table from Nicholas Love's Mirror, and a fragmentary translation of Oseney Abbey's Latin cartulary. The register compilation contains charters and related records on the abbey's foundation, privileges, waterways, chapels, and Oxfordshire estates; Clark organizes its 279 editorial entry units under 33 title groups.

AHA2740 encodes selected matter from Clark's two-volume edition of the composite TNA E 164/26 source. The historical target begins with an independent, abruptly ending title and chapter-table fragment of Nicholas Love's Mirror and continues with an incomplete English translation of the Oseney cartulary. Clark's 279 register entry containers are grouped beneath 33 title headings; they are documentary/editorial units, not 279 separately cataloged literary works.

The Mirror fragment supplies a title and chapter headings through an incomplete thirtieth chapter heading. The register translates charters and related records concerning Oseney Abbey's foundation, transfer of St George's, meadows, waterways, chapels, and estates across Oxfordshire, ending in the Barford group during entry 278. Clark's modern persons/places/matters and words/phrases indexes follow in the encoded back matter.

extent.description: Intellectual-item total: two prose historical components, comprising one unnumbered partial Mirror chapter-table fragment and one incomplete English register/documentary compilation. Structural/editorial metrics remain separate: the register has 279 DIV3 entry units under 33 title groups, numbered 1-278 with interpolated 117*A. Those units are not counted as separate intellectual items and are not asserted to be 279 independently bounded physical deeds. Parent/root framing and two modern indexes count zero. The XML contains 9 DIV1, 33 DIV2, 279 DIV3, 322 PB, 121 MILESTONE, 2442 P, no L, and no LG.

content_parts.label: The English Register of Oseney Abbey edition-level composite
content_parts.summary: Root container for Clark's framing, the independent Mirror chapter-table fragment, the translated Oseney register, and the two encoded modern indexes.

content_parts.label: Edition framing and selectively encoded front matter
content_parts.summary: Part wrappers, title pages, contents, and omission placeholders derived from Clark's two issue parts; the U-M page-image sequence exposes additional forewords and grammatical matter not transcribed as continuous XML text.

locators.value: front-matter containers before the historical target
1-68; sequence 68 is the blank transition immediately before the Mirror title

content_parts.label: The Mirrour of the Lyfe of Cryste: title and partial chapter table
content_parts.summary: An independent unfoliated fragment prefixed to the register: the Mirror title and chapter headings grouped by days, breaking during the thirtieth heading. It is neither the continuous devotional text nor a complete contents table.

locators.value: THE MIRROURE OF THE LYFE OF CRYSTE

notes: The fragment's recurrence of a prefixed table and day grouping supports identification with Nicholas Love's Mirror tradition; exact recension and affiliation remain unresolved.

content_parts.label: Oseney Cartulary Englished
content_parts.summary: Incomplete Middle English translation of the Oseney Latin register, organized by Clark into 33 title groups and 279 entry containers. It follows Christ Church D&C vi.a.1 (= MS 343) through approximately Latin f. 93v and ends abruptly during entry 278.

locators.value: OSENEY CARTULARY ENGLISHED; 33 DIV2 and 279 DIV3
numbered ff. 1r-61r; ff. 61v-65v blank before the later Latin section
translation follows the Latin register from its beginning through approximately f. 93v

notes: The 279 DIV3 units are treated as documentary/editorial entries within one translated register collection, not as 279 independently cataloged works.
Title XXXIII is absent in Clark's sequence; the edition leaves open whether a group is missing or a numeral was dropped.

content_parts.label: V. Foundation of St George's
content_parts.summary: First register title group, containing entries 1-10.

content_parts.label: VII. St George's given to Oseney
XIX. Hensington
content_parts.summary: Register title group containing entries 116-125 plus interpolated entry 117*A.

content_parts.label: Rowlesham
content_parts.summary: Unnumbered register title group containing entries 173-176.
notes: This heading has no Roman title number in Clark's sequence.

content_parts.label: XXXIV. Hook Norton
notes: Clark's sequence jumps from XXXII to XXXIV; no XXXIII heading occurs in the English text.

content_parts.label: XXXVII. Barford
content_parts.summary: Final register title group, containing entries 273-278 and ending abruptly in the last deed.

content_parts.label: Index I: Persons, places, and matters
content_parts.summary: Clark's modern editorial index to names, places, and subjects represented in the text.

content_parts.label: Index II: Words and phrases
content_parts.summary: Clark's modern lexical and phrase index.

related_works.title: The Mirror of the Blessed Life of Jesus Christ
notes: AHA2740 contains only a title and partial chapter-table fragment of this abstract work, not a complete Mirror witness.

related_works.title: Meditationes vitae Christi
notes: This relation is scoped through the Nicholas Love fragment. The Latin work's authorship is disputed; Bonaventure is not asserted as its preferred modern author.

related_works.title: Latin Cartulary of Oseney Abbey in Christ Church D&C vi.a.1 (= MS 343)
notes: The Middle English register follows this second Latin register's order through approximately f. 93v. Cotton MS Vitellius E XV is an earlier related register used only for selected comparison readings in Clark's edition.

evidence.citation: General Corpus, CME/source/CME_phase_1-2/aha2740.xml, pinned from Kyvero-Press/CME commit e1d77c88ca561be924b2fedc54dd474be2e7e49c.
evidence.summary: Supplies the electronic identifier, source-edition statements, Mirror/register boundary, 33 register title groups, 279 entry containers, pagination, foliation, selective front/back encoding, and U-M encoding declaration.

evidence.citation: Element, heading, entry-number, page, and folio audit of the pinned aha2740 XML against Clark's scans and U-M's complete IIIF sequence.
evidence.locator: All DIV1-DIV3, PB, MILESTONE, HEAD, P, NOTE1, L, and LG elements; all 322 IIIF canvases
evidence.summary: Counts 9 DIV1, 33 DIV2, 279 DIV3, 322 PB, 121 MILESTONE, 2442 P, no L, and no LG; maps sequence 68 as the blank transition, the Mirror to printed pp. [1]-4/sequences 69-72, the register to pp. 5-209/sequences 73-277, sequence 278 as blank [210], and Index I to printed pp. [211]-214/sequences 279-282; verifies all group counts and the 1-278 plus 117*A numbering pattern.

evidence.citation: Andrew Clark, ed., The English Register of Oseney Abbey, Part I: Text, Early English Text Society Original Series 133 (London, 1907), complete Internet Archive scan.
evidence.locator: title and contents; printed pp. [1]-209; especially Mirror pp. [1]-4 and register pp. 5-209
evidence.summary: Verifies the complete target text, the fragmentary Mirror chapter table, 33 register title groups, 279 entries, manuscript folio milestones, supplied Roman title numerals, notes, and the register's abrupt ending.

evidence.citation: Andrew Clark, ed., The English Register of Oseney Abbey, Part II: Introduction, Grammar, and Indexes, Early English Text Society Original Series 144 (London, 1913), complete Internet Archive scan.
evidence.locator: forewords on the composite manuscript and Latin registers; grammar; printed pp. [211]-234 indexes
evidence.summary: Describes the three-section TNA E 164/26 host, dates and boundaries the prefixed Mirror and English register fragments, identifies the English register's Christ Church Latin source, credits Salter's transcript and selected Cotton readings, and supplies the two encoded indexes.

evidence.citation: The National Archives, E 164/26, Cartulary of Osney Abbey, Oxon, Discovery record C2960649.
evidence.locator: reference, title, date, description, closure, legal status, digitisation status, and edition note
evidence.summary: Confirms E 164/26 as an extant fifteenth-sixteenth-century Oseney cartulary held at Kew, open but not digitised, with Clark's edition explicitly limited to fols. 1-61.

evidence.citation: Christ Church, Oxford, D&C vi.a.1 (= MS 343), Cartulary of Oseney Abbey, MARCO record ark:/29072/c09593tt47vq.
evidence.locator: summary, royal identification, date, contents, origin, language, support, extent, and additional-history notes
evidence.summary: Identifies the second Latin Oseney cartulary as a late-thirteenth-century Oxford manuscript, perhaps 1280-1284, with the cartulary at ff. 9r-343v and additions through the sixteenth century; this is Clark's stated source register for the English translation.

evidence.citation: Geographies of Orthodoxy, University of Queen's Belfast, corpus profile for Nicholas Love's Mirror of the Blessed Life of Jesus Christ.
evidence.summary: Identifies Nicholas Love as translator and adapter of the Middle English Mirror from the pseudo-Bonaventuran Meditationes vitae Christi and distinguishes the uncertain Latin authorship from the older Bonaventure attribution.

evidence.citation: Geographies of Orthodoxy, manuscript affiliations and corpus description for Nicholas Love's Mirror.
evidence.locator: contents-table, day-grouping, manuscript-form, and dating discussion
evidence.summary: Shows that Mirror witnesses normally prefix a contents table and group chapters by days, matching the formal features of the E 164/26 fragment; it also supports a date around 1410 for Love's work.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library Digital Collections, AHA2740.0001.001 IIIF Presentation manifest and complete 322-canvas image sequence.
evidence.summary: Provides a complete current page-image sequence for the combined U-M object. Direct inspection maps sequence 68 as a blank transition, the Mirror to sequences 69-72, the register to 73-277, sequence 278 as blank [210], Index I to 279-282, Index II to 283-302, and sequence 303 as the start of advertisements; it also confirms that the image carrier includes front/back matter omitted from continuous XML transcription.

open_questions.question: Which textual recension and manuscript family of Nicholas Love's Mirror does the E 164/26 title and partial chapter table represent?
next_steps: Obtain images of the prefixed unfoliated section and collate every surviving heading against current Mirror manuscript inventories.
Do not inherit Clark's John Morton or Bonaventure shorthand as preferred modern authorship.

open_questions.question: How do Clark's 279 entry containers and 33 title groups map to the manuscript's physical deed boundaries, and does the missing XXXIII represent lost content or only a dropped numeral?
next_steps: Inspect TNA E 164/26 ff. 1r-61r against Clark's rubric and entry divisions.
Keep entry counts editorial/documentary rather than claiming 279 physically independent documents until direct comparison is complete.

open_questions.question: Can the English register translation be dated and localized more precisely than Clark's approximate 1460?
next_steps: Consult current palaeographical and dialectological study of E 164/26.
Keep copy date, translation date, institutional association, and current custody separate.

open_questions.question: Does U-M's current No Copyright-United States statement supersede the conditional availability notice embedded in the 2006 XML, and does it cover the current IIIF page-image derivatives?
next_steps: Ask U-M Digital Collections for written clarification before redistributing the transcription, markup, or cached IIIF image bundle.
Keep the medieval texts, Clark edition, modern scans, U-M encoding, U-M page images, and repository artifact as distinct rights components.

notes: Content structure is complete only at Clark/U-M edition and XML title-group granularity; physical deed boundaries in TNA E 164/26 have not been verified.
Record status is partial because exact Mirror affiliation, physical deed boundaries, and several reproduction/rights questions require mediated manuscript access.
The complete U-M image carrier and complete Clark volume scans are cached; work portions identify target locations without implying that the cached files were cropped.
The full TNA E 164/26 host includes a later Latin papal-confirmation section at ff. 66r-112v outside the cataloged target.
